baseline_score: Without this skill, Claude posts a top-level summary comment with no inline placement, combines all findings into one block, and does not submit a formal review event (approve/request-changes).

evals.description: PR with regression and incomplete fix — should detect blockers and REQUEST_CHANGES
evals.setup: PR unifies RBAC condition matching. Has: deleted integration test masking regression, $in null guard missing while $nin/$ne are patched, claims in CHANGELOG that don't match code.
evals.expected_output: Posts inline 🔴 Blocker comments for the regression and the claim mismatch. Submits REQUEST_CHANGES. Does not APPROVE.
expectations: Detects the deleted testEvaluateMatchConditionsResolvedRelation test as a masked regression
Identifies that operatorIn lacks a null guard while operatorNotIn has one
Posts each blocker as a separate inline comment on the correct file and line
Submits REQUEST_CHANGES formal review
Does NOT post blockers and concerns combined in one comment

evals.description: PR with concerns but no blockers — should APPROVE with concern comments
evals.prompt: Review PR #999 (hypothetical clean PR with minor issues)
evals.setup: PR has no regressions, no claim mismatches. Has: one missing @deprecated tag, one DocBlock that overstates the consolidation.
evals.expected_output: Posts 🟡 Concern inline comments. Submits APPROVE. Does not REQUEST_CHANGES.
expectations: Correctly identifies no merge-blocking issues
Posts concerns as separate inline 🟡 comments
Submits APPROVE with a one-sentence summary
Summary does not claim the PR is perfect — notes what concerns were raised

evals.description: PR with 150KB+ diff — should use isolated analysis delegate and not exceed context
evals.prompt: Review a PR with 18 changed files and 2500 additions
evals.setup: Diff is ~156KB. Context window would overflow if diff is embedded inline.
evals.expected_output: Saves diff to /tmp/, passes the file path to an isolated analysis context, does not paste full diff into chat.
expectations: Saves diff to temp file before analysis
Analysis delegate receives file path, not raw diff content
Analysis covers all 18 changed files, not just the first few
Comment line numbers are verified against diff hunks

evals.description: Inline comments land on valid diff lines
evals.prompt: Review PR and post inline comments
evals.setup: PR has multiple hunks. Some target lines are in context; others are added lines.
evals.expected_output: All inline comments land on real diff lines. GitHub API does not return 422.
expectations: Uses @@ hunk headers to compute new-file line numbers
Does not comment on lines outside the diff
Verifies comments after posting via gh api pulls/comments
If a target line is not in the diff, moves comment to nearest context line with a note

evals.description: Re-running review on same PR does not duplicate comments
evals.prompt: Review a PR that already has review comments from a previous run
evals.setup: PR already has 3 inline comments posted by a prior review run.
evals.expected_output: Does not post duplicate comments. Either skips or asks the user.
expectations: Checks existing comments before posting
Does not post the same finding twice
Reports to user that comments already exist

evals.description: Re-review with no new commits or comments — should stop early
evals.prompt: Review a PR that was already reviewed and has had no changes since
evals.setup: PR has a prior review from the current user. No commits, no PR comments, no thread replies since that review.
evals.expected_output: Stops early and tells the user there is nothing to re-review.
expectations: Fetches the user's prior review via gh api pulls/{n}/reviews
Fetches commits and finds none after last_review.submitted_at
Fetches PR comments and inline replies and finds none after last_review.submitted_at
Reports: 'No new commits or comments since your last review on {date}. Nothing to re-review.'
Does NOT proceed to ask about strictness or post any comments

evals.description: Re-review where prior comments were addressed — replies to resolved threads
evals.prompt: Re-review a PR where the author addressed the blockers from the first review
evals.setup: PR has prior review with 2 blocker comments. New commits fix both issues. No new blockers.
evals.expected_output: Replies to each resolved comment with '✅ Resolved in {sha}: explanation'. Submits APPROVE.
expectations: Identifies new commits pushed after last review
Matches resolved issues to prior comment IDs
Posts '✅ Resolved in {sha}:' replies via gh api pulls/comments/{id}/replies
Does NOT repost the original blocker as a new finding
Submits APPROVE since all blockers are resolved

evals.description: Auth or RBAC code → recommends Strict mode
evals.prompt: Review a PR touching authentication middleware
evals.setup: PR modifies auth middleware, permission checks, and session handling.
evals.expected_output: Asks the user about strictness and recommends Strict based on security-sensitive code.
expectations: Detects security/auth/RBAC-related file changes
Recommends 'Strict' in the AskUserQuestion prompt
Provides a brief reason (e.g. 'security-sensitive code touched')
Proceeds with the user-chosen mode, not the recommended one if overridden

evals.description: Quick mode — approve with concerns, don't REQUEST_CHANGES
evals.prompt: Do a quick review of this small config PR
evals.setup: User selects Quick mode. PR has 2 concerns and 0 blockers.
evals.expected_output: Posts 0 concern comments (concerns skipped in Quick mode). Submits APPROVE.
expectations: Skips 🟡 Concern and 🟢 Minor findings — does not post them
Submits APPROVE since no definite 🔴 blockers exist
Does not REQUEST_CHANGES due to concerns in Quick mode

evals.prompt: Do a strict review of this RBAC PR
evals.setup: User selects Strict mode. PR has 0 blockers, 2 concerns.
evals.expected_output: Posts all concerns as inline comments. Submits REQUEST_CHANGES because concerns exist in Strict mode.
expectations: Posts all 🟡 Concern findings as inline comments
Submits REQUEST_CHANGES (not APPROVE) despite zero 🔴 blockers
Verdict body notes that concerns require resolution in Strict mode

should_trigger: please do a code review on this PR
review the changes in this pull request
do a PR review for me
check this PR and post comments: https://github.com/foo/bar/pull/42
I need a review of pull request 234
give me feedback on this PR
review PR #1340 and submit request changes if needed
should_not_trigger: review the code in my editor
check CI status on the PR
assign a reviewer to the PR
how different is this PR from the previous one
